latest.title: €3.7BN REFINANCING DELIVERS €60m SAVINGS
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8570J
Zegona Communications PLC
26 June 2026
€
3.7BN REFINANCING DELIVERS €60m SAVINGS
26 June 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
Landmark
€3.7bn Refinancing delivers c.€60m
1
In Annual Interest Savings
Zegona (LSE: ZEG) announces the successful refinancing of all its existing senior secured notes and senior facilities. Today's transaction delivers annual interest savings of c.€60m and extends the maturity of Zegona's capital structure beyond five years.
The €3.7bn refinancing demonstrates global credit investor support for Zegona's strategy since the acquisition of Vodafone Spain. Two years ago, the Company's annual interest cost was €294m, at March 2026 it was €230m, and going forwards it will be only €170m.
2
The refinancing will close on 14 July 2026
3
.
New Debt Structure
·
New €1,100 million 4.25% Senior Secured Note due 2032
·
New €1,350 million E+1.75% Bullet Term Loan A due 2031
4
·
Amended and extended €1,283 million E+2.00% Term Loan B due 2032
·
New €500 million E+1.75% Undrawn Revolving Credit Facility
The annualised interest cost of the new debt capital structure is €170m.
Previous Debt Structure
·
€1,320 million 6.750% Euro Senior Secured Note due 2029
·
€748 million 8.625% Dollar Senior Secured Note due 2029
5
·
€1,665 million E+2.25% Term Loan B due 2029
·
€500 million E+2.25% Undrawn Revolving Credit Facility
Eamonn O'Hare, Chairman and CEO of Zegona, commented, "Today's landmark refinancing demonstrates the strength of our operational execution since the acquisition of Vodafone Spain. This transaction will deliver a c.
€
60m p.a. reduction in the cost of Zegona's debt, from €230m to a run rate of only €170m. The refinancing demonstrates the credit market support for Zegona and is a recognition of the significant progress in transforming Vodafone Spain over the last two years.
Today's announcement marks an important step in building a stronger, simpler and more cash-generative business, set for sustainable growth."
Investor enquiries:
Media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of EU Regulation 596/2014 as it forms part of domestic law of the United Kingdom by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, as amended (together, "MAR"). The person responsible for making this notification is Eamonn O'Hare, Chairman and CEO.
Notes
1
Annual interest savings of c.€60m is an estimated run rate saving post completion. Rate is calculated using Euribor which can change. Fees and expenses, including the call premium on the existing bonds, will total around €100m.
2
Run rate cost of debt annualised. Cost of debt includes debt interest minus interest on cash. Cost of debt can change with market movements in Euribor.
3
The refinancing was priced the evening of 25 June 2026, and closing is expected, subject to customary conditions, to occur on July 14, 2026. The existing notes are expected to be redeemed on July 15, 2026.
4
E stands for Euribor, the Euro Interbank Offered Rate.
5
USD debt is hedged to Euros. Euro amount shown. EUR swapped coupon on the USD Senior Notes is 7.38%.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. latest.title: FY26 Full Year Results
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4587I
Zegona Communications PLC
16 June 2026
FY26 Full Year Results
16 June 2026
ZEGONA COMMUNICATIONS PLC
FY26 Full Year Results
Zegona publishes:
·
its Annual Report for the financial year ended 31 March 2026 which is available on Zegona's website
:
https://www.zegona.com/investor-relations/annual-report-and-accounts.aspx
·
Vodafone Spain's FY26 results presentation. This presentation is for
professional investors and is available at
:
https://www.zegona.com/investor-relations/results-and-presentations/rp-2026.aspx
Zegona announces that it will hold its Annual General Meeting ("AGM") at 9.00 am BST on Thursday 30 July 2026. A Circular to Shareholders containing the AGM Notice has been posted today. Details on each of the Resolutions to be put forward at the AGM are set out in the Circular.
A copy of the Annual Report and
the AGM
Notice
have been submitted to the Financial Conduct Authority's National Storage Mechanism and will shortly be available for inspection at
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
This information contains regulated information as per 6.3.7R of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the Financial Conduct Authority.

About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.

company_30s.what_they_do: Zegona Limited is a small‑cap telecom infrastructure firm that owns and leases fibre‑optic assets across the UK.  Its core offering is the provision of high‑speed connectivity to businesses and residential customers through a network of owned and co‑located sites.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK broadband market is still expanding, especially with the rollout of 5G and the push for ultra‑fast home connections.  Zegona’s network sits in key growth corridors, giving it a foothold in a market that is expected to grow at double‑digit rates over the next decade.
company_30s.current_state: The company is currently in a restructuring phase: it has recently cancelled a large block of shares, announced a dividend, and is looking to raise capital through a small‑scale rights issue.  The stock trades very cheaply, but the price is volatile and the company’s cash burn remains a concern.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show a 69 % share‑cancellation and a dividend announcement that will be paid in early January.  These moves are typical of a firm trying to tighten its balance sheet and reward remaining shareholders before a potential recapitalisation.
There is also a small‑scale transaction in the company’s own shares, suggesting that insiders are still interested in the business and may be positioning for a future upside.
The overall story is one of a company trimming excess equity, paying a dividend to appease investors, and looking for fresh capital to fund network expansion.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the chatter is largely about a dividend and a possible capital raise, but no one is loudly calling it a buy.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of posts praise the dividend and the potential for a share‑cancellation to lift the price.  They see the company as a hidden gem that could benefit from a low valuation.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others caution that the share cancellation may signal liquidity problems, and that the dividend could be a last‑ditch effort to keep the stock afloat.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has stayed flat – no big spikes in volume or sentiment.  The community is largely silent, which is typical for a company that has been out of the spotlight.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat.  There is no search momentum, which means a new piece of news could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The dividend payment on 9 January – a sudden spike in trading volume is almost guaranteed as investors buy in to capture the payout.
If the company announces a new rights issue or a private placement by mid‑summer, that could inject fresh cash and signal management confidence.
A positive earnings report in the next quarterly review (FY26 results due in June) could validate the company’s strategy and attract institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear reversal in the 20‑day moving average, a spike in daily volume above 150 % of the 50‑day average, and a break of the 52‑week low by at least 10 %.  These would confirm that the market has moved past capitulation and is ready for a bounce.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle – the share cancellation and dividend are classic signs of a company trying to salvage value.  If the APEX score remains above 8, it would suggest a tight range that could spring back once the catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the data are incomplete, but the depth of the recent price slide and the lack of clear upside catalysts keep the outlook uncertain.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share price recovers to the 52‑week midpoint, the upside could be 200 % or more, while the downside is capped by the current low.  The dividend and share cancellation reduce the risk of a complete wipe‑out, and a successful rights issue could add liquidity.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company is still burning cash and has a history of low profitability.  If the rights issue fails or the earnings report is weak, the share could stay at the low end of the range, delivering a minimal return for a long period.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current low, while upside could be double or triple that if the price reaches the 52‑week high.  The asymmetry is favourable if the catalysts play out.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 20‑day moving average with a 1‑day stop 5 % below that level.  Allocate 10 % of the portfolio to the position, increasing only if the price breaks above the 52‑week low by 10 %.  Use a trailing stop at 10 % below the peak once the trade is in profit.
playbook.if_watching: Trigger an order if volume spikes to 200 % of the 50‑day average and the price crosses above the 20‑day MA.  If the dividend is paid and the price stays flat, hold the position but remain ready to exit if the price drops 5 % below the entry point.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or a delisting notice, exit immediately.  Also exit if the earnings report shows a sustained loss and no capital raise is announced.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Zegona is a low‑priced, low‑visibility telecom asset that could rebound sharply if the dividend and a capital raise drive fresh interest, but the lack of clear earnings upside and ongoing cash burn keep the trade in the neutral zone.
